text: Apple Inc. (AAPL) reported revenue of $94.8 billion for Q3 2024, representing a 5% increase year-over-year. CEO Tim Cook attributed the growth to strong iPhone sales in emerging markets and continued expansion of the Services segment, which generated $21.2 billion in revenue.

The European Central Bank held its benchmark interest rate steady at 4.5% on Thursday, as President Christine Lagarde signaled that inflation in the eurozone remains above the 2% target. The decision was widely anticipated by analysts at Deutsche Bank and Barclays.

Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) announced the acquisition of gaming company Activision Blizzard (ATVI) for approximately $68.7 billion in an all-cash transaction. The deal, expected to close in fiscal year 2024, would make Microsoft the third-largest gaming company by revenue.

JPMorgan Chase (JPM) filed its quarterly 10-Q report with the SEC on November 14, 2024, disclosing net income of $13.2 billion for the quarter. CFO Jeremy Barnum noted that the bank maintained a Common Equity Tier 1 ratio of 15.3%, well above the regulatory minimum.

Goldman Sachs Group analysts forecast that the S&P 500 could reach 5,200 by the end of 2024, driven by improving corporate earnings and potential Federal Reserve rate cuts. Chief equity strategist David Kostin highlighted technology and healthcare sectors as the strongest growth areas.

Tesla Inc. (TSLA) shares fell 8.3% to $215.49 on Tuesday after the company reported delivering 435,059 vehicles in Q2 2024, missing Wall Street estimates of 448,000 units. Revenue for the quarter came in at $24.9 billion, a 2% decline from the prior year.

Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) announced plans to invest $4 billion in Anthropic, an artificial intelligence startup, as part of a broader strategy to compete with Microsoft and Google in the AI infrastructure market. The investment will be funded in USD over the next two years.

The Bank of Japan maintained its ultra-loose monetary policy on Friday, keeping the short-term interest rate at -0.1% and the 10-year Japanese government bond yield target near 0%. Governor Kazuo Ueda stated that inflation at 2.8% remains driven by temporary factors and the yen weakened to 149.5 per USD following the announcement.

Pfizer Inc. (PFE) reported Q4 2024 earnings per share of $0.63, beating consensus estimates of $0.48 by 31%. Total revenue of $14.2 billion was boosted by strong demand for its oncology portfolio, while COVID-related product revenue declined 42% year-over-year to $2.8 billion.

Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) disclosed in its annual 13-F filing that it had reduced its holdings in Bank of America (BAC) by 15% during Q3 2024, selling approximately 85 million shares valued at $2.5 billion. Chairman Warren Buffett did not comment on the rationale for the sale.
